headlines.title: Stealing from the gods: India’s Ram Temple hit by corruption scandal
headlines.snippet: India's Ram Temple is facing a corruption scandal with embezzlement allegations. This is embarrassing for PM Modi's government, especially ahead of crucial state elections.

headlines.title: Skydiving plane crash kills 11 in northeastern France
headlines.snippet: A plane belonging to a parachuting school crashed in Tomblaine, France, resulting in the deaths of 11 people.

headlines.title: Thousands of Kurds gather in Turkiye to demand release of PKK leader
headlines.snippet: Thousands of Kurds gathered in Turkiye to demand the release of PKK leader Abdullah Öcalan and other prisoners.

headlines.title: Oil prices rise as US, Iranian strikes threaten Strait of Hormuz reopening
headlines.snippet: Oil prices have increased as strikes involving the US and Iran threaten the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway.

headlines.title: Australian man charged with murder after Thai girl’s body found in suitcase
headlines.snippet: An Australian man, Simon Peter Carman, has been charged with murder following the discovery of the body of 17-year-old Tunchanok Donhomla in a suitcase.

headlines.title: Saudi Crown Prince and Macron discuss US-Iran agreement, peace efforts
headlines.snippet: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and French Prime Minister Emmanuel Macron discussed a Memorandum of Understanding between the US and Iran, along with regional developments. They also emphasized the importance of freedom of navigation and diplomatic efforts to reduce tensions.

headlines.title: Morning update
headlines.snippet: Reports suggest the United States and Iran are returning to diplomacy, with an agreement to halt military strikes and resume talks in Doha. Communication channels remain active despite unresolved tensions over the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Iranian minister discusses Hormuz management with Omani counterpart
headlines.snippet: Iran's deputy foreign minister, Kazem Gharibabadi, discussed the future management of the Strait of Hormuz with Oman's Minister of State for Foreign Affairs, Abdulaziz Al-Hinai. The exchange occurred within the framework of an MoU and coastal states' sovereign rights.

headlines.title: Israeli cyber chief reports sharp rise in Iranian cyberattacks after war
headlines.snippet: A senior Israeli security official stated that Iranian cyberattacks against Israel have sharply increased since the US-Israeli offensive. The number of incidents rose significantly from approximately 1,600 in June 2025 to about 4,800 in June 2026.

headlines.title: Residents return after Israeli forces withdraw from Syrian village
headlines.snippet: Residents have returned to a Syrian village after Israeli forces withdrew from Tal al-Maghar hill in the western Deraa countryside. This withdrawal followed Syria's condemnation of Israeli incursions and shelling in Deraa and Quneitra.

headlines.title: Israel destroys Hezbollah underground infrastructure in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Israel has reported the destruction of underground Hezbollah infrastructure located in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: Iran canceled participation in technical talks over recent attacks, state TV reports
headlines.snippet: State TV reports indicate that Iran has canceled its participation in technical talks due to recent attacks.

headlines.title: Turkey denounces Israel’s ‘political’ recognition of Armenia genocide
headlines.snippet: Turkey has denounced Israel’s recognition of the Armenian genocide as 'political'.

headlines.title: Death toll from Venezuela quakes rises to 1,450
headlines.snippet: The death toll from earthquakes in Venezuela has risen to 1,450.

headlines.title: Qatar says citizen killed from shrapnel due to ‘military operations’
headlines.snippet: Qatar has stated that one of its citizens was killed by shrapnel resulting from 'military operations'.

headlines.title: UK's likely next leader Burnham to unveil plan to shift power
headlines.snippet: An individual named Burnham, expected to be the UK's next leader, is set to reveal a plan focused on industry, housing, and infrastructure amidst rising political tensions within the Labour party.

headlines.title: Israel detonates tunnel, strikes south Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces have detonated a tunnel in southern Lebanon, and Hezbollah has warned that it reserves the right to retaliate against continued strikes despite a ceasefire.

headlines.title: Pakistan says carried out operation, strikes along Afghan border
headlines.snippet: Pakistan has announced that its forces killed 29 militants during intelligence-based operations and airstrikes along the Afghanistan border, targeting militant hideouts.

headlines.title: Iran warns ships not to bypass its chosen Hormuz route
headlines.snippet: Iran has issued a warning against ships bypassing its designated route through the Strait of Hormuz, amid fears of renewed Middle East tensions due to US-Iran attacks.

headlines.title: Kurds rally in Turkey's southeast for jailed PKK leader
headlines.snippet: Thousands of Kurds rallied in Diyarbakir, a city in Turkey with a Kurdish majority, to advocate for the release of Abdullah Ocalan, who is currently imprisoned.

headlines.title: History will not forget perpetrators, supporters of Sardasht chemical bombing: Iran envoy
headlines.snippet: Iran's ambassador to Germany stated that history will remember not only those responsible for the chemical bombing of Sardasht City but also those who facilitated it through technology, equipment, or silence.

headlines.snippet: Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei described the holy city of Karbala as a place embodying the empathy and affection between the Iraqi and Iranian people.

headlines.title: In photos: Reshadat; Persian Gulf’s largest oil processing platform
headlines.snippet: The P4 processing platform at Iran’s Reshadat oil field, one of the country's largest, has been successfully installed in the Persian Gulf. This structure was designed and built by Iranian specialists and domestic manufacturers.

headlines.title: Foreign dignitaries to begin arriving in Iran on Friday for martyred Leader’s funeral: VP
headlines.snippet: First Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref announced that foreign officials and delegations will arrive in Iran on Friday to attend the farewell and funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei.

headlines.title: Iran demands justice over Sardasht chemical attack on 39th anniversary
headlines.snippet: Marking the 39th anniversary of the Sardasht chemical bombing, Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei reaffirmed Iran's pursuit of justice for victims and accountability for perpetrators and their foreign backers.

headlines.title: Will gold prices continue to decline?

headlines.title: France reports nearly 1,000 more deaths than usual due to searing heatwave
headlines.snippet: French health authorities reported that nearly 1,000 more deaths than usual have occurred since Wednesday due to the heatwave. Temperatures have exceeded 40C in many areas.

headlines.title: Uganda’s military chief orders shutdown of two major media outlets
headlines.snippet: Uganda's military chief has ordered the closure of two major media outlets, stating he does not believe in a free press. The affected outlets are the Daily Monitor and NTV Uganda.

headlines.title: Israel’s military and tech industry race to counter Hezbollah’s latest threat
